column1: # UCDP Georeferenced Event Dataset (GED) 23.1 indicator catalog
# Source: UCDP GED 23.1 CSV zip
# License: free academic; cite UCDP per https://ucdp.uu.se/terms-of-use/
# Citation: Davies
#   Allansson
#   Uppsala Conflict Data Program. https://ucdp.uu.se/downloads/
# Attribution: see docs/sources/attributions.md
# This file is the single source of truth for which UCDP indicators are
# read in Stage 2. Every Stage 2 UCDP adapter call resolves its aggregation
# from this file. Adding/removing an indicator here is the only change needed
# to widen or narrow the UCDP ingestion for the prototype.
# Architecture design: docs/architecture/ucdp.md §2.4
# Aggregation strategy:
#   The UCDP GED is an event-level dataset (316
#   Stage 2 aggregates events to country-year by type_of_violence:
#     - type=1: state-based conflict (two organized groups
#     - type=2: non-state conflict (two non-state groups) — NOT on the catalog
#     - type=3: one-sided violence (state/organized group vs civilians)
#   For each country-year
#     - event_count: count of events after the type filter
#     - best: sum of the UCDP 'best' fatalities estimate column
#   The 'best' column is UCDP's point estimate of total deaths (the sum of
#   deaths_a + deaths_b + deaths_civilians + deaths_unknown when all four are
#   known; UCDP's best-estimate methodology fills gaps using high/low bounds).
# Internationalized conflict filter (the gwnob column):
#   UCDP populates the 'gwnob' column (Gleditsch-Ward state number for side_b)
#   when side_b is a foreign state. A non-null gwnob marks a cross-border
#   event. Live probe of v23.1 shows gwnob is non-null for 6
#   state-based events (2.7%) — the genuine internationalized subset.
#   Filter for ucdp_intl_* indicators: type_of_violence == 1 AND gwnob.notna()
# Convention for extending this catalog:
#   Add one row per new indicator. The 'filter_logic' column holds the pandas
#   query string used at read time; the 'raw_column' field holds the semantic
#   aggregation ('event_count' = count rows
#   Do not invent new raw_column values without updating ucdp_io.py.
# Scale:
#   All 6 indicators normalize to 0-1 in Stage 5 (the score module handles
#   the log-transform for fatalities). All 6 have higher_is_better=False
#   (more violence = worse rating).
variable_name
ucdp_state_based_events
column8: Number of state-based violent events (government vs non-state or inter-state) per country-year. Higher = more armed conflict.

column1: ucdp_state_based_fatalities
column8: Total deaths in state-based events per country-year (UCDP best estimate). Higher = more lethal armed conflict.

column1: ucdp_intl_events
column8: Number of state-based events involving a foreign state (cross-border / internationalized). Subset of state-based. Higher = more internationalized conflict.

column1: ucdp_intl_fatalities
column8: Total deaths in internationalized state-based events per country-year. Subset of state-based fatalities.

column1: ucdp_onesided_events
column8: Number of one-sided violence events (government or organized group vs civilians) per country-year. Higher = more state-perpetrated violence against civilians.

column1: ucdp_onesided_fatalities
column8: Total deaths in one-sided violence events per country-year. Higher = more lethal state-perpetrated civilian violence.
